rulings.Case Title: BERRY VS XU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 29, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT LIN-XING XU'S Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution; Request For Monetary Sanctions. MOTION TO DISMISS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT THE STAY WAS NOT OPERATIVE DURING THE SIX YEAR PERIOD ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. SANCTIONS FOR FAILURE TO PARTICIPATE IN JUDICIAL ARBITRATION ARE ADDRESSED IN THE CIVIL CASE MANAGEMENT DEPARTMENT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH KINNARD VS. PAT'S LIEN SERVICE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Jdugment Order Failure To Call 4pm Temative Ruling Excusable Neglect. Presentation Of New Evidence Under 14th And 1st Amendment

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. LUXOR CAB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 29, 2009, line 10, PLAINTIFF PATRICK HELLAND'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED. THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED ON A PUBLIC STREET AFTER PLAINTIFF'S SHIFT ENDED AS HE WAS ATTEMPTING TO COMMUTE HOME. NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE SUBMITTED SHOWING THAT THE ACCIDENT ACTUALLY OCCURRED ON DEFENDANT'S PROPERTY. JERROLD AVENUE IS A PUBLIC THOROUGHFARE AND THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT USED THE STREET'S PARKING DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THE NECESSARY CONTROL. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THE SPECIAL RISK EXCEPTION APPLIES. NO SHOWING THAT THE "DANGER IS ONE TO WHICH THE EMPLOYEE, BY REASON OF AND IN CONNECTION WITH HIS EMPLOYMENT, IS SUBJECTED PARTICULARLY OR TO AN ABNORMAL DEGREE. NO RISK IDENTIFIED THAT IS "DISTINCTIVE IN NATURE OR QUANTITATIVELY GREATER THAN RISKS COMMON TO THE PUBLIC." GENERAL INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA V. WORKERS' COMP. APPEALS BOARD (1976) 16 CAL.3D 595. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BURNS TOMMY et al VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 29, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ALLEGATIONS RAISED IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF WERE DECIDED IN STRAUSS V. HORTON (2009) 46 CAL.4TH 364. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: C & J LEASING INC et al VS. MOHSEN HASSEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs C & J Leasing, Inc. And Royal Taxi, Inc.'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 09/24/09)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 29, 2009, line 14, Plaintiffs C & J Leasing, Inc. And Royal Taxi, Inc.'s Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. MOTION DENIED. THE LEASE DOES NOT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AGAINST DEFENDANT'S TRANSFER OF THE NEW MEDALLION. THE LEASE IS CLEARLY FOR USE OF THE PERMIT/MEDALLION #9063, NOT #1381. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS MAY HAVE A CLAIM THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED THE LEASE SINCE DEFENDANT DID NOT MAINTAIN MEDALLION #9063 IN GOOD STANDING, THE LEASE IS SILENT AS TO ANY CHANGE IN THE MEDALLION. PLAINTIFFS ALSO FAIL TO SHOW INADEQUATE LEGAL REMEDY. THE LEASE AWARDS MONEY DAMAGES FOR REVOCATION OF THE MEDALLION, AND IF PLAINTIFF PREVAILS, LOST REVENUE IS SUBJECT TO CALCULATION. =(302/CWW)
